name: org.machanism.machai:project-layout
description: Project Layout is a small Java utility library for describing, detecting, and processing conventional project directory layouts across multiple ecosystems. It provides a uniform API for build tooling, automation, plugins, repository scanners, and code generation workflows that need to locate project roots, source folders, test folders, documentation folders, and nested modules reliably. The library centers on the org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout abstraction, which models a project root together with root-relative paths and optional child modules. It includes ecosystem-specific implementations for Maven, Gradle, JavaScript and TypeScript workspaces, Python projects, and a generic filesystem fallback. The org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectLayoutManager entry point detects the appropriate layout implementation by inspecting project metadata such as pom.xml, build.gradle, package.json, pyproject.toml, setup.py, and setup.cfg. The org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor abstraction supports recursive traversal of projects and nested modules, delegating concrete processing logic to subclasses while reusing consistent layout discovery. Maven-specific support includes pom.xml parsing, source and resource directory discovery, test directory discovery, parent and module resolution for packaging pom projects, and Maven model parsing through org.machanism.machai.project.layout.PomReader. The library is designed for infrastructure and utility use cases such as repository analysis, documentation processing, build automation, plugin development, project inspection, and tooling that must operate consistently across heterogeneous project structures.
license: Commercial / Proprietary (copyright ©2025-2026 Machanism.org; specific open-source license not stated in the analyzed Javadoc)

usageContext: Detecting project layout from a filesystem directory
Resolving conventional source, test, and documentation folders
Supporting Maven, Gradle, JavaScript workspace, Python, and generic projects
Feeding project metadata into plugins, generators, or analysis tools

constructors.description: Creates a Maven-specific layout resolver that reads pom.xml metadata to determine modules, source directories, resource directories, test roots, documentation inputs, project identifiers, and parent information.

examples.description: Creates a MavenProjectLayout, binds it to a project directory, and then queries conventional source, test, and documentation locations for use in build or scanning logic.

constructors.description: Creates the fallback layout implementation used when no ecosystem-specific project metadata can be detected. It performs lightweight filesystem inspection and treats immediate subdirectories as potential modules while returning safe defaults for sources, tests, and documents.

examples.description: Uses the generic fallback layout when a repository does not expose Maven, Gradle, JavaScript, or Python metadata, allowing tools to still enumerate candidate module folders.

constructors.description: Creates a Gradle-specific layout resolver that uses the Gradle Tooling API to expose child projects together with conventional src/main, src/test, and src/site directories.

examples.description: Initializes a Gradle layout model and queries modules and documentation roots for a multi-project Gradle build.

constructors.description: Creates a JavaScript and TypeScript workspace layout resolver that reads package.json and resolves workspaces into module paths while exposing conventional project folders.

examples.description: Constructs the JS workspace layout helper and retrieves workspace modules and the package name from package.json.

constructors.description: Creates a Python project layout helper that detects Python project indicators such as pyproject.toml, setup.py, setup.cfg, requirements.txt, and common environment conventions, while returning safe defaults until deeper folder discovery is implemented.

examples.description: Initializes Python project detection and allows callers to work with the common ProjectLayout API even when source and documentation discovery are intentionally conservative.

constructors.description: Creates a helper for parsing Maven pom.xml files into Maven model objects, applying lightweight property replacement, exposing parsed properties, and supporting model serialization.

examples.description: Creates a PomReader to parse a pom.xml file and then reads both the Maven model and the map of resolved POM properties for custom tooling workflows.

features.name: detectProjectLayout
features.signature: public static org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout detectProjectLayout(java.io.File projectDir) throws java.io.FileNotFoundException
features.description: Detects the effective project layout for a filesystem directory by inspecting build and ecosystem metadata. According to the Javadoc, it supports Maven, Node.js package.json projects, Python projects, and falls back to a generic layout when no specialized convention applies. This method is the main entry point for callers that want one uniform API regardless of repository technology.

examples.description: How to use it: point the library at any project directory, let it detect the correct layout implementation, and then consume the unified ProjectLayout API to obtain source, test, and documentation folders without hardcoding build-tool-specific rules.

features.name: scanFolder
features.signature: public void scanFolder(java.io.File projectDir) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Scans a main project directory, detects whether modules are present, and processes each module recursively when necessary. For leaf projects, it delegates to subclass-defined folder processing logic. This enables reusable repository traversal pipelines that separate traversal concerns from business-specific processing.

examples.description: How to use it: implement ProjectProcessor.processFolder to define what should happen for each detected leaf project, then call scanFolder on a repository root. The processor will recursively traverse modules and invoke your logic only when a concrete project layout is ready.

features.name: getProjectLayout
features.description: Returns the detected ProjectLayout for a specified project directory. This is useful when custom processors need direct access to layout detection without reimplementing the logic used by ProjectLayoutManager.

examples.description: How to use it: from a custom ProjectProcessor implementation, obtain the detected layout for a target directory and inspect project metadata such as identifiers, sources, tests, and modules.

features.name: getModules
features.description: Provides module discovery for layout implementations. In MavenProjectLayout it returns modules declared in pom.xml when packaging is pom. In GragleProjectLayout it exposes child Gradle projects. In JScriptProjectLayout it resolves package.json workspaces into module paths. In DefaultProjectLayout it treats immediate subdirectories as potential modules. This is central for tools that need to understand monorepos or multi-module builds.

examples.description: How to use it: after detecting a layout, ask for modules to determine whether the target is a parent or monorepo-style project. This supports recursive scanning, module-specific reporting, and selective automation.

features.name: getSources
features.description: Returns root-relative production source directories for the detected project type. MavenProjectLayout reads Maven build metadata and defaults, GragleProjectLayout returns src/main, and other layouts provide either conventional or safe-default results. The returned values are designed to be resolved against the project root before file access.

examples.description: How to use it: detect a layout, retrieve root-relative source folders, then resolve them against the project root to feed compilers, scanners, generators, or documentation processors.

features.name: getTests
features.description: Returns root-relative test directories for the active layout. MavenProjectLayout includes test source and test resource directories, GragleProjectLayout returns src/test, and other layouts expose safe defaults. This helps tools separate production inputs from validation assets.

examples.description: How to use it: query test roots when building test-specific scanners, QA pipelines, mutation analysis tooling, or reports that distinguish production code from test code.

features.name: getDocuments
features.description: Returns root-relative documentation directories for the active layout. Maven and Gradle layouts report src/site as the conventional documentation location, while other layouts return safe defaults. This enables consistent discovery of documentation inputs for site generation or analysis tools.

examples.description: How to use it: locate documentation folders such as src/site in a build-tool-neutral way before generating static sites, aggregating docs, or preparing documentation processing jobs.

features.name: getProjectModel
features.description: Parses a Maven pom.xml file into a Maven model after applying lightweight property substitutions. This feature is useful for tools that need structured Maven metadata without reimplementing low-level parsing.

examples.description: How to use it: parse pom.xml into a structured Maven model, then read artifact metadata, build sections, modules, parent information, or other Maven coordinates for custom automation.

features.name: getPomProperties
features.description: Returns properties parsed from pom.xml files. This allows callers to inspect values resolved during Maven model processing and reuse them in custom reporting or secondary configuration logic.

examples.description: How to use it: first parse a pom.xml file, then inspect the collected Maven properties to power diagnostics, configuration export, or property-driven automation tasks.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor.processFolder
customizations.description: Primary extension point for custom repository-processing workflows. Developers subclass ProjectProcessor and implement processFolder(ProjectLayout) to define the logic that should run for each detected leaf project layout after module traversal has been handled.

examples.description: How to use it: implement the abstract method to customize what happens for each discovered project, such as indexing source roots, generating docs, running analyzers, or collecting metadata.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout
customizations.description: Abstract base type for creating new ecosystem-specific layout resolvers. Developers can extend this class to support additional build systems or organizational conventions while preserving the same API for sources, tests, documents, modules, and project identifiers.

examples.description: How to use it: extend ProjectLayout to model a new project convention, then expose root-relative folders through the standard API so existing processing code can work with the new layout type.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout
studs.description: Base abstraction for describing a project's conventional on-disk layout. It defines the contract for root-relative source, test, and documentation folders, optional modules, project identifiers, relative path utilities, and shared filesystem scanning behavior.

examples.description: Shows the abstract ProjectLayout being consumed polymorphically through a concrete implementation so callers can write layout-neutral tooling.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor
studs.description: Abstract base class for processing project structures and modules. It provides reusable recursive scanning and delegates concrete folder handling to subclasses via processFolder(ProjectLayout).
methods: public void scanFolder(java.io.File projectDir) throws java.io.IOException
protected void processModule(java.io.File projectDir, java.lang.String module) throws java.io.IOException

examples.description: Demonstrates implementing the abstract processor to build a reusable repository traversal tool that prints project names for each discovered leaf project.

examples.title: Install as a Maven dependency and detect a project layout
examples.scenario: Use Project Layout as a Java library inside a build tool, plugin, or repository analysis application.
steps: Add the library dependency to your Maven project using coordinates org.machanism.machai:project-layout:1.2.0-SNAPSHOT.
Create a java.io.File pointing to the project or repository root you want to inspect.
Call org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectLayoutManager.detectProjectLayout(java.io.File) to let the library select the correct layout implementation.
Read source, test, documentation, and module information from the returned org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout object.
Resolve returned root-relative paths against the project root before performing file operations.
examples.snippet:
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.machanism.machai</groupId>
  <artifactId>project-layout</artifactId>
  <version>1.2.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
</dependency>

java.io.File projectDir = new java.io.File("C:\\workspace\\target-project");
org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout layout = org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectLayoutManager.detectProjectLayout(projectDir);
System.out.println(layout.getProjectLayoutType());
System.out.println(layout.getSources());
System.out.println(layout.getTests());
System.out.println(layout.getDocuments());
examples.description: This example shows a practical how-to-use-it flow for library consumers: install the dependency, detect the project layout automatically, and query standardized folder metadata without writing build-tool-specific detection code.

examples.title: Process a multi-module repository with a custom ProjectProcessor
examples.scenario: Build a reusable repository scanner that works across Maven, Gradle, JavaScript workspace, Python, and fallback layouts.
steps: Install the library as a dependency in your Java application or plugin.
Create a subclass or anonymous implementation of org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor.
Implement processFolder(org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout) with your custom processing logic, for example collecting source roots or documentation roots.
Invoke scanFolder(java.io.File) on the repository root.
Let the processor recurse through modules automatically and run your custom logic for each leaf project.
examples.description: This example demonstrates an end-to-end usage scenario where a developer installs the library, configures a custom processor, and runs it against a real repository to traverse modules and analyze layout information.

examples.title: Read Maven metadata with PomReader
examples.scenario: Use the Maven helper utilities when you specifically need parsed pom.xml information in addition to general layout detection.
steps: Add the project-layout dependency to your Java application.
Pass a java.io.File pointing to pom.xml into getProjectModel(java.io.File).
Inspect the returned org.apache.maven.model.Model for artifact metadata.
Optionally inspect resolved POM properties through getPomProperties().
examples.description: This how-to-use-it scenario focuses on the Maven-specific parsing helper and shows how to install, configure, and run the utility in a concrete metadata extraction workflow.
